summary: User asks composer to process inline customer rows and route them to two named sinks based on a row predicate. Tests gate-routing path; exercises the routes field on a transform/gate node and two distinct sinks. Re-authored 2026-05-07 (Tier 1.5 final hardening) using inline content — the original prompt referenced /tmp paths which the composer correctly refuses (Trust Tier 3 boundary). The workspace input contract requires create_blob + set_source_from_blob (skill Pattern 1b) or inline_blob in set_pipeline.
opening_prompt:
Please create a pipeline that processes the following customer rows and routes them by status. Rows where status is 'active' should go to a sink at outputs/active.jsonl, everything else to a sink at outputs/inactive.jsonl. Customer rows (CSV):
name,status
alice,active
bob,inactive
charlie,active
dan,pending
eve,active

red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Same passivity catch-list as url-download-line-explode. Anti-tail-offer rule applies across all scenarios.
build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.build_failure_note: Server-injected from service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() on completion-claim with failed preflight.

green_criteria.must_have_outputs_min_note: Routing must produce two named sinks. One-sink solutions are RED.
